skill.name: add-malli-schemas
skill.description: Guide for adding Malli request and response schemas to Metabase API endpoints using established project patterns.
skill.summary: Add Malli schemas to Metabase API endpoints with correct validation timing, reusable schema patterns, and focused tests.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many execution, credential, crypto, reconnaissance, and obfuscation patterns, but review found a Markdown guide with Clojure examples rather than executable skill code. The remaining risks are low: one optional local REPL command and one external documentation link.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The repeated external command findings are Markdown inline code spans and fenced Clojure examples. They document API schema patterns and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are visibly Markdown documentation and Clojure snippets. No script file, hook, or automatic execution path exists in the scanned skill.

low_findings.title: Optional Local REPL Command Mentioned
low_findings.description: The guide suggests a local ./bin/mage REPL command for inspecting Clojure documentation. This requires deliberate user execution and does not include remote input or automatic command launch.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is a real shell command in a bash code block, but it is instructional and local. Risk is limited to normal developer review before running project commands.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Link Present
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to public Malli documentation. It is a reference link and does not send local data or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is plainly displayed as documentation, with no surrounding code that performs a network request. The only concern is normal link trust.

low_findings.title: Credential Access Match Is a Filename
low_findings.description: The env_access finding is caused by a reference file path containing api_keys. No environment variables, token reads, or secret extraction logic were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a Markdown list of Metabase source files. It does not access process environments, key stores, or credential values.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation, Crypto, and Reconnaissance Heuristics Dismissed
low_findings.description: The blocker and obfuscation findings map to Markdown front matter, Malli vector syntax, schema examples, headings, and prose. No encoded payload, cryptographic operation, or host reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary documentation syntax at the cited lines. There is no code path that hides behavior, probes the system, or invokes weak cryptography.

content.user_title: Add Malli Schemas to Metabase APIs
content.value_statement: Metabase API endpoints need consistent request and response validation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the Malli patterns used in the Metabase codebase.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to add route, query, body, and response schemas to Metabase endpoints.
Shows when to use inline schemas versus reusable named schemas.
Documents Metabase Malli utility types such as positive integers, nonblank strings, and boolean values.
Clarifies request validation versus response validation timing in the Metabase API lifecycle.
Lists common schema mistakes for nullable fields, optional parameters, temporal values, and sets.
Recommends checking implementation returns and tests before finalizing response schemas.
limitations: The skill is specific to Metabase Clojure API conventions.
It does not inspect a repository or modify files by itself.
The examples may need adaptation for endpoints with unusual return values.
Users must run tests and review generated schemas before merging changes.

use_cases.title: Add Validation to an Existing Endpoint
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to add route, query, body, and response schemas to one Metabase endpoint.

use_cases.title: Review Schema Choices in a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Compare proposed schemas with documented Metabase patterns for optional fields, nullable values, and response timing.

use_cases.title: Learn Metabase Malli Conventions
use_cases.description: Study practical examples of named schemas, custom error messages, pagination responses, and schema utility types.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Schemas
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Malli schemas to this Metabase API endpoint. Start with route parameters, query parameters, request body, and response schema.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Reusable Schemas
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this endpoint and identify which response structures should become named Malli schemas instead of inline schemas.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Validation Timing Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: This Metabase response schema fails before JSON serialization. Check temporal fields, sets, keywords, and nullable values.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Schema Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Malli schema migration for Metabase conventions, error clarity, optional parameters, reusable types, and missing tests.

output_examples.input: A Metabase endpoint needs validation for an ID route parameter and optional query parameters.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies separate schema locations, recommends Metabase utility types, and notes which query parameters need optional markers or defaults.

output_examples.input: A response schema rejects Java time values before JSON serialization.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains response validation timing and recommends accepting the internal value shape before middleware serializes it.

output_examples.input: A pull request repeats the same nested response map in several endpoints.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a named schema, explains the reuse benefit, and suggests targeted tests for valid and invalid responses.

best_practices: Inspect the endpoint implementation and tests before writing response schemas.
Prefer existing Metabase Malli utility types when they match the API value.
Test valid requests, invalid requests, optional parameters, and error responses.
anti_patterns: Do not combine route, query, and body parameters into one schema map.
Do not use request-only temporal string schemas for Java time response values.
Do not make optional query parameters required by omitting optional markers or defaults.

faq.question: What codebase is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is designed for Metabase Clojure API endpoints that use Malli schemas.

faq.question: Can it modify files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that an assistant or developer can apply.

faq.question: Which schema types should I prefer?
faq.answer: Prefer existing Metabase Malli utility types when they represent the same value constraints.

faq.question: Why do response schemas differ from request schemas?
faq.answer: Responses are validated before JSON serialization, so schemas must match internal Clojure values.

faq.question: Does it cover custom validation errors?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for contextual API error messages and documented enum schemas.

faq.question: How should I verify schema changes?
faq.answer: Run focused tests for valid inputs, invalid inputs, optional parameters, and response validation.
